title: Kohleria longifolia
taxonomy: Kohleria longifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the "African violet family". The Gesneriaceae family includes over 1,900 species of plants, many of which are epiphytes or terrestrial plants native to tropical regions. Kohleria longifolia is native to Central America. The classification of Kohleria longifolia places it within the genus Kohleria, which contains species of flowering plants that are similar in appearance and growth habit.

The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. intro: Kohleria longifolia, also known as "Long-leaved Kohleria," is a species of flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Kohleria longifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kohleria longifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
